name: LiteTV Channels
description: Lightweight virtual TV channels: deterministic schedules over your own library, tuned in via normal direct playback at the live position. No transcoding, no tuner emulation.
overview: TV channels from your library, without transcoding.

versions.changelog: Channels can interleave their sources in blocks: set 'Episodes per block' (e.g. 2) so two series air alternating, S1E1, S1E2, S2E1, S2E2, ... (0 keeps the marathon behaviour). Watch state is now fully protected: each item's played flag, play count and resume position are snapshotted before playback and restored on stop and on leaving, so channel viewing never marks anything watched. Returning to the home page now also leaves the channel cleanly.

Channels no longer stop after one item: the schedule now follows continuously from item to item (Jellyfin swaps media in place without a view change, which the previous watcher missed). The TV home-screen row is placed in the normal sections flow, removing the large gap above it. Fixes a tuned-state leak when leaving the channel after an in-place advance.

The header 📺 button now has its own setting, independent of the home row and overlays, so it can be hidden while the TV row and playback overlays stay on. 'Enable the TV UI' remains the master switch.

Skipping ahead no longer stops the channel: the schedule keeps following item to item even when episodes are finished early. The follow-up now also starts precisely at the credits using Jellyfin's Media Segments (outro) when available, falling back to a short margin before the end otherwise, so the tail credits are skipped.

The TV home-screen row no longer flickers when returning to the home page: the channel guide is cached and the row is only rebuilt when the schedule actually changes. The end-of-episode overlay also hides its choice buttons when following the schedule and continuing the series lead to the same next episode.

TV row stays at the bottom of the home screen; pause panel synced with Jellyfin Enhanced's pause screen and positioned top-right; end-of-episode overlay hides its buttons when there is no series to continue.

Overlay buttons no longer toggle play/pause; Next Up suppressed while tuned; watch state fully untouched; skipping ahead advances the schedule; earlier end-of-episode overlay; pause panel (Jellyfin Enhanced integration); channel suggestions in the settings; polished visuals; plugin icon.
Initial release: continuous-loop channels, live tune-in in the web client, end-of-episode continue/schedule overlay, watch-state hygiene, play-on-device for native clients.
